title: Navigation History, Immutable Snapshots, Back, and Bounded Undo
description: History stores mutable references, pushes attempts before success, back triggers new hidden network work, unlimited pages accumulate, and undo can resurrect a stale pending request. Learners cumulatively produce bounded immutable navigation timeline through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define back as prior admitted view, not arithmetic or browser history, and expose when no snapshot exists.
Store readonly copied domain snapshots or stable cache identities without shared mutable arrays or pending controllers.
Push only after success, restore without hidden traffic when promised, and cancel or supersede pending operations safely.
